authors_text: Md Yousuf Harun,Jhair Gallardo,Tyler L. Hayes,Ronald Kemker,Christopher Kanan
title: SIESTA: Efficient Online Continual Learning with Sleep
title_zh: SIESTA：通过睡眠高效在线持续学习
abstract: In supervised continual learning, a deep neural network (DNN) is updated with an ever-growing data stream. Unlike the offline setting where data is shuffled, we cannot make any distributional assumptions about the data stream. Ideally, only one pass through the dataset is needed for computational efficiency. However, existing methods are inadequate and make many assumptions that cannot be made for real-world applications, while simultaneously failing to improve computational efficiency. In this paper, we propose a novel continual learning method, SIESTA based on wake/sleep framework for training, which is well aligned to the needs of on-device learning. The major goal of SIESTA is to advance compute efficient continual learning so that DNNs can be updated efficiently using far less time and energy. The principal innovations of SIESTA are: 1) rapid online updates using a rehearsal-free, backpropagation-free, and data-driven network update rule during its wake phase, and 2) expedited memory consolidation using a compute-restricted rehearsal policy during its sleep phase. For memory efficiency, SIESTA adapts latent rehearsal using memory indexing from REMIND. Compared to REMIND and prior arts, SIESTA is far more computationally efficient, enabling continual learning on ImageNet-1K in under 2 hours on a single GPU; moreover, in the augmentation-free setting it matches the performance of the offline learner, a milestone critical to driving adoption of continual learning in real-world applications.
summary: In supervised continual learning, a deep neural network (DNN) is updated with an ever-growing data stream. Unlike the offline setting where data is shuffled, we cannot make any distributional assumptions about the data stream. Ideally, only one pass through the dataset is needed for computational efficiency. However, existing methods are inadequate and make many assumptions that cannot be made for real-world applications, while simultaneously failing to improve computational efficiency. In this paper, we propose a novel continual learning method, SIESTA based on wake/sleep framework for training, which is well aligned to the needs of on-device learning. The major goal of SIESTA is to advance compute efficient continual learning so that DNNs can be updated efficiently using far less time and energy. The principal innovations of SIESTA are: 1) rapid online updates using a rehearsal-free, backpropagation-free, and data-driven network update rule during its wake phase, and 2) expedited memory consolidation using a compute-restricted rehearsal policy during its sleep phase. For memory efficiency, SIESTA adapts latent rehearsal using memory indexing from REMIND. Compared to REMIND and prior arts, SIESTA is far more computationally efficient, enabling continual learning on ImageNet-1K in under 2 hours on a single GPU; moreover, in the augmentation-free setting it matches the performance of the offline learner, a milestone critical to driving adoption of continual learning in real-world applications.
abstract_zh: 在监督持续学习中，深度神经网络 (DNN) 随着不断增长的数据流进行更新。与数据被打乱的离线设置不同，我们不能对数据流做出任何分布假设。理想情况下，为了提高计算效率，只需要遍历一次数据集。然而，现有方法存在不足，并且做出了许多无法用于实际应用的假设，同时无法提高计算效率。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的持续学习方法——基于唤醒/睡眠框架进行训练的SIESTA，它很好地满足了设备上学习的需求。 SIESTA 的主要目标是推进计算高效的持续学习，以便可以使用更少的时间和精力高效更新 DNN。 SIESTA 的主要创新是：1）在唤醒阶段使用免排练、免反向传播和数据驱动的网络更新规则进行快速在线更新，2）在睡眠阶段使用计算限制的排练策略加速内存整合。为了提高记忆效率，SIESTA 使用 REMIND 中的记忆索引来调整潜在排练。与 REMIND 和现有技术相比，SIESTA 的计算效率要高得多，可以在单个 GPU 上在 2 小时内连续学习 ImageNet-1K；此外，在无增强设置中，它与离线学习者的表现相匹配，这是推动在现实世界应用中采用持续学习的关键里程碑。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SIESTA方法，通过醒睡框架实现高效在线持续学习，减少计算资源消耗。
AI.motivation: 现有持续学习方法在真实场景中因假设过多且计算效率低下而不足，需开发无需回放、反向传播的高效算法以支持设备端学习。
AI.method: SIESTA采用醒睡框架：醒阶段使用无回放、无反向传播的数据驱动更新规则；睡阶段通过受限回放策略加速记忆巩固，并结合REMIND的记忆索引实现潜隐回放。
AI.result: SIESTA在单GPU上2小时内完成ImageNet-1K持续学习，且在无数据增强设置下性能匹配离线学习器，计算效率远超REMIND等方法。
AI.conclusion: SIESTA通过创新框架显著提升持续学习的计算效率，为真实场景应用提供了关键性能保障。

tldr: 本文提出SIESTA方法，通过醒睡框架实现高效在线持续学习，减少计算资源消耗。
motivation: 现有持续学习方法在真实场景中因假设过多且计算效率低下而不足，需开发无需回放、反向传播的高效算法以支持设备端学习。
method: SIESTA采用醒睡框架：醒阶段使用无回放、无反向传播的数据驱动更新规则；睡阶段通过受限回放策略加速记忆巩固，并结合REMIND的记忆索引实现潜隐回放。
result: SIESTA在单GPU上2小时内完成ImageNet-1K持续学习，且在无数据增强设置下性能匹配离线学习器，计算效率远超REMIND等方法。
conclusion: SIESTA通过创新框架显著提升持续学习的计算效率，为真实场景应用提供了关键性能保障。
search_text: SIESTA: Efficient Online Continual Learning with Sleep SIESTA：通过睡眠高效在线持续学习 In supervised continual learning, a deep neural network (DNN) is updated with an ever-growing data stream. Unlike the offline setting where data is shuffled, we cannot make any distributional assumptions about the data stream. Ideally, only one pass through the dataset is needed for computational efficiency. However, existing methods are inadequate and make many assumptions that cannot be made for real-world applications, while simultaneously failing to improve computational efficiency. In this paper, we propose a novel continual learning method, SIESTA based on wake/sleep framework for training, which is well aligned to the needs of on-device learning. The major goal of SIESTA is to advance compute efficient continual learning so that DNNs can be updated efficiently using far less time and energy. The principal innovations of SIESTA are: 1) rapid online updates using a rehearsal-free, backpropagation-free, and data-driven network update rule during its wake phase, and 2) expedited memory consolidation using a compute-restricted rehearsal policy during its sleep phase. For memory efficiency, SIESTA adapts latent rehearsal using memory indexing from REMIND. Compared to REMIND and prior arts, SIESTA is far more computationally efficient, enabling continual learning on ImageNet-1K in under 2 hours on a single GPU; moreover, in the augmentation-free setting it matches the performance of the offline learner, a milestone critical to driving adoption of continual learning in real-world applications. 在监督持续学习中，深度神经网络 (DNN) 随着不断增长的数据流进行更新。与数据被打乱的离线设置不同，我们不能对数据流做出任何分布假设。理想情况下，为了提高计算效率，只需要遍历一次数据集。然而，现有方法存在不足，并且做出了许多无法用于实际应用的假设，同时无法提高计算效率。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的持续学习方法——基于唤醒/睡眠框架进行训练的SIESTA，它很好地满足了设备上学习的需求。 SIESTA 的主要目标是推进计算高效的持续学习，以便可以使用更少的时间和精力高效更新 DNN。 SIESTA 的主要创新是：1）在唤醒阶段使用免排练、免反向传播和数据驱动的网络更新规则进行快速在线更新，2）在睡眠阶段使用计算限制的排练策略加速内存整合。为了提高记忆效率，SIESTA 使用 REMIND 中的记忆索引来调整潜在排练。与 REMIND 和现有技术相比，SIESTA 的计算效率要高得多，可以在单个 GPU 上在 2 小时内连续学习 ImageNet-1K；此外，在无增强设置中，它与离线学习者的表现相匹配，这是推动在现实世界应用中采用持续学习的关键里程碑。 本文提出SIESTA方法，通过醒睡框架实现高效在线持续学习，减少计算资源消耗。 Md Yousuf Harun,Jhair Gallardo,Tyler L. Hayes,Ronald Kemker,Christopher Kanan cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Devendra K. Jangid,Neal R. Brodnik,McLean P. Echlin,Tresa M. Pollock,Samantha H. Daly,B. S. Manjunath
title: Q-RBSA: High-Resolution 3D EBSD Map Generation Using An Efficient Quaternion Transformer Network
title_zh: Q-RBSA：使用高效四元数变压器网络生成高分辨率 3D EBSD 地图
abstract: Gathering 3D material microstructural information is time-consuming, expensive, and energy-intensive. Acquisition of 3D data has been accelerated by developments in serial sectioning instrument capabilities; however, for crystallographic information, the electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) imaging modality remains rate limiting. We propose a physics-based efficient deep learning framework to reduce the time and cost of collecting 3D EBSD maps. Our framework uses a quaternion residual block self-attention network (QRBSA) to generate high-resolution 3D EBSD maps from sparsely sectioned EBSD maps. In QRBSA, quaternion-valued convolution effectively learns local relations in orientation space, while self-attention in the quaternion domain captures long-range correlations. We apply our framework to 3D data collected from commercially relevant titanium alloys, showing both qualitatively and quantitatively that our method can predict missing samples (EBSD information between sparsely sectioned mapping points) as compared to high-resolution ground truth 3D EBSD maps.
summary: Gathering 3D material microstructural information is time-consuming, expensive, and energy-intensive. Acquisition of 3D data has been accelerated by developments in serial sectioning instrument capabilities; however, for crystallographic information, the electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) imaging modality remains rate limiting. We propose a physics-based efficient deep learning framework to reduce the time and cost of collecting 3D EBSD maps. Our framework uses a quaternion residual block self-attention network (QRBSA) to generate high-resolution 3D EBSD maps from sparsely sectioned EBSD maps. In QRBSA, quaternion-valued convolution effectively learns local relations in orientation space, while self-attention in the quaternion domain captures long-range correlations. We apply our framework to 3D data collected from commercially relevant titanium alloys, showing both qualitatively and quantitatively that our method can predict missing samples (EBSD information between sparsely sectioned mapping points) as compared to high-resolution ground truth 3D EBSD maps.
abstract_zh: 收集 3D 材料微观结构信息非常耗时、昂贵且耗能。串行切片仪器功能的发展加速了 3D 数据的获取；然而，对于晶体学信息，电子背散射衍射 (EBSD) 成像模式仍然受到速率限制。我们提出了一种基于物理的高效深度学习框架，以减少收集 3D EBSD 地图的时间和成本。我们的框架使用四元数残差块自注意力网络 (QRBSA) 从稀疏截面的 EBSD 图生成高分辨率 3D EBSD 图。在 QRBSA 中，四元数值卷积有效地学习方向空间中的局部关系，而四元数域中的自注意力则捕获长程相关性。我们将我们的框架应用于从商业相关钛合金收集的 3D 数据，定性和定量地表明，与高分辨率地面实况 3D EBSD 图相比，我们的方法可以预测缺失样本（稀疏剖面映射点之间的 EBSD 信息）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于四元数变换器网络的高效深度学习框架，用于生成高分辨率3D电子背散射衍射（EBSD）图。
AI.motivation: 传统3D材料微观结构信息获取耗时耗资，而EBSD成像模式在晶体学信息采集速度上存在瓶颈，需开发高效方法降低时间和成本。
AI.method: 设计四元数残差块自注意力网络（QRBSA），利用四元数卷积学习取向空间局部关系，通过四元数域自注意力机制捕捉长程相关性，从稀疏截面EBSD图生成高分辨率3D地图。
AI.result: 在商用钛合金3D数据集上验证，该方法在定性和定量指标上均优于高分辨率真实3D EBSD图，可有效预测稀疏截面间的缺失EBSD信息。
AI.conclusion: QRBSA框架通过物理感知的深度学习方法，实现了从稀疏数据生成高分辨率3D EBSD图，为晶体学信息采集提供了高效解决方案。

Jangid,Neal R. Brodnik,McLean P. Echlin,Tresa M. Pollock,Samantha H. Daly,B. S. Manjunath cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Kathy Reid,Elizabeth T. Williams
title: Right the docs: Characterising voice dataset documentation practices used in machine learning
title_zh: 正确的文档：表征机器学习中使用的语音数据集文档实践
comment: 16 pages, 3 tables, preprint of a submission to AIES 2023
comments: 16 pages, 3 tables, preprint of a submission to AIES 2023
abstract: Voice-enabled technology is quickly becoming ubiquitous, and is constituted from machine learning (ML)-enabled components such as speech recognition and voice activity detection. However, these systems don't yet work well for everyone. They exhibit bias - the systematic and unfair discrimination against individuals or cohorts of individuals in favour of others (Friedman & Nissembaum, 1996) - across axes such as age, gender and accent. ML is reliant on large datasets for training. Dataset documentation is designed to give ML Practitioners (MLPs) a better understanding of a dataset's characteristics. However, there is a lack of empirical research on voice dataset documentation specifically. Additionally, while MLPs are frequent participants in fairness research, little work focuses on those who work with voice data. Our work makes an empirical contribution to this gap. Here, we combine two methods to form an exploratory study. First, we undertake 13 semi-structured interviews, exploring multiple perspectives of voice dataset documentation practice. Using open and axial coding methods, we explore MLPs' practices through the lenses of roles and tradeoffs. Drawing from this work, we then purposively sample voice dataset documents (VDDs) for 9 voice datasets. Our findings then triangulate these two methods, using the lenses of MLP roles and trade-offs. We find that current VDD practices are inchoate, inadequate and incommensurate. The characteristics of voice datasets are codified in fragmented, disjoint ways that often do not meet the needs of MLPs. Moreover, they cannot be readily compared, presenting a barrier to practitioners' bias reduction efforts. We then discuss the implications of these findings for bias practices in voice data and speech technologies. We conclude by setting out a program of future work to address these findings -- that is, how we may "right the docs".
summary: Voice-enabled technology is quickly becoming ubiquitous, and is constituted from machine learning (ML)-enabled components such as speech recognition and voice activity detection. However, these systems don't yet work well for everyone. They exhibit bias - the systematic and unfair discrimination against individuals or cohorts of individuals in favour of others (Friedman & Nissembaum, 1996) - across axes such as age, gender and accent. ML is reliant on large datasets for training. Dataset documentation is designed to give ML Practitioners (MLPs) a better understanding of a dataset's characteristics. However, there is a lack of empirical research on voice dataset documentation specifically. Additionally, while MLPs are frequent participants in fairness research, little work focuses on those who work with voice data. Our work makes an empirical contribution to this gap. Here, we combine two methods to form an exploratory study. First, we undertake 13 semi-structured interviews, exploring multiple perspectives of voice dataset documentation practice. Using open and axial coding methods, we explore MLPs' practices through the lenses of roles and tradeoffs. Drawing from this work, we then purposively sample voice dataset documents (VDDs) for 9 voice datasets. Our findings then triangulate these two methods, using the lenses of MLP roles and trade-offs. We find that current VDD practices are inchoate, inadequate and incommensurate. The characteristics of voice datasets are codified in fragmented, disjoint ways that often do not meet the needs of MLPs. Moreover, they cannot be readily compared, presenting a barrier to practitioners' bias reduction efforts. We then discuss the implications of these findings for bias practices in voice data and speech technologies. We conclude by setting out a program of future work to address these findings -- that is, how we may "right the docs".
abstract_zh: 支持语音的技术正在迅速普及，由语音识别和语音活动检测等支持机器学习 (ML) 的组件组成。然而，这些系统尚未适合所有人。他们在年龄、性别和口音等方面表现出偏见，即对个人或群体进行系统性和不公平的歧视，以偏袒他人（Friedman & Nissembaum，1996）。机器学习依赖于大型数据集进行训练。数据集文档旨在让机器学习从业者 (MLP) 更好地理解数据集的特征。然而，缺乏专门针对语音数据集文档的实证研究。此外，虽然 MLP 经常参与公平性研究，但很少有工作关注那些处理语音数据的人。我们的工作对这一差距做出了实证贡献。在这里，我们结合两种方法进行探索性研究。首先，我们进行了 13 次半结构化访谈，探索语音数据集文档实践的多个视角。使用开放和轴向编码方法，我们通过角色和权衡的角度探索 MLP 的实践。根据这项工作，我们有目的地对 9 个语音数据集的语音数据集文档 (VDD) 进行采样。然后，我们的研究结果使用 MLP 角色和权衡的视角对这两种方法进行了三角测量。我们发现当前的 VDD 实践还不成熟、不充分且不相称。语音数据集的特征以碎片化、不相交的方式进行编码，这通常不能满足 MLP 的需求。此外，它们不能轻易进行比较，这给从业者减少偏见的努力带来了障碍。然后，我们讨论这些发现对语音数据和语音技术中的偏见实践的影响。最后，我们制定了一个未来工作计划来解决这些发现——也就是说，我们如何“纠正文档”。

AI.tldr: 本文通过实证研究分析语音数据集文档实践，揭示其不足并提出改进方向以减少语音技术中的偏见。
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏对语音数据集文档的实证分析，且忽视了语音数据从业者在公平性研究中的角色，需填补这一空白。
AI.method: 结合13次半结构化访谈（采用开放和轴向编码分析角色与权衡）及9个语音数据集文档的针对性采样，通过三角验证方法进行研究。
AI.result: 当前语音数据集文档实践碎片化、不完整且难以比较，无法满足从业者需求，成为减少偏见的障碍。
AI.conclusion: 提出改进文档实践的未来研究方向，即通过系统化、标准化的文档规范来优化语音技术的公平性。

result: 当前语音数据集文档实践碎片化、不完整且难以比较，无法满足从业者需求，成为减少偏见的障碍。
conclusion: 提出改进文档实践的未来研究方向，即通过系统化、标准化的文档规范来优化语音技术的公平性。
search_text: Right the docs: Characterising voice dataset documentation practices used in machine learning 正确的文档：表征机器学习中使用的语音数据集文档实践 Voice-enabled technology is quickly becoming ubiquitous, and is constituted from machine learning (ML)-enabled components such as speech recognition and voice activity detection. However, these systems don't yet work well for everyone. They exhibit bias - the systematic and unfair discrimination against individuals or cohorts of individuals in favour of others (Friedman & Nissembaum, 1996) - across axes such as age, gender and accent. ML is reliant on large datasets for training. Dataset documentation is designed to give ML Practitioners (MLPs) a better understanding of a dataset's characteristics. However, there is a lack of empirical research on voice dataset documentation specifically. Additionally, while MLPs are frequent participants in fairness research, little work focuses on those who work with voice data. Our work makes an empirical contribution to this gap. Here, we combine two methods to form an exploratory study. First, we undertake 13 semi-structured interviews, exploring multiple perspectives of voice dataset documentation practice. Using open and axial coding methods, we explore MLPs' practices through the lenses of roles and tradeoffs. Drawing from this work, we then purposively sample voice dataset documents (VDDs) for 9 voice datasets. Our findings then triangulate these two methods, using the lenses of MLP roles and trade-offs. We find that current VDD practices are inchoate, inadequate and incommensurate. The characteristics of voice datasets are codified in fragmented, disjoint ways that often do not meet the needs of MLPs. Moreover, they cannot be readily compared, presenting a barrier to practitioners' bias reduction efforts. We then discuss the implications of these findings for bias practices in voice data and speech technologies. We conclude by setting out a program of future work to address these findings -- that is, how we may "right the docs". 支持语音的技术正在迅速普及，由语音识别和语音活动检测等支持机器学习 (ML) 的组件组成。然而，这些系统尚未适合所有人。他们在年龄、性别和口音等方面表现出偏见，即对个人或群体进行系统性和不公平的歧视，以偏袒他人（Friedman & Nissembaum，1996）。机器学习依赖于大型数据集进行训练。数据集文档旨在让机器学习从业者 (MLP) 更好地理解数据集的特征。然而，缺乏专门针对语音数据集文档的实证研究。此外，虽然 MLP 经常参与公平性研究，但很少有工作关注那些处理语音数据的人。我们的工作对这一差距做出了实证贡献。在这里，我们结合两种方法进行探索性研究。首先，我们进行了 13 次半结构化访谈，探索语音数据集文档实践的多个视角。使用开放和轴向编码方法，我们通过角色和权衡的角度探索 MLP 的实践。根据这项工作，我们有目的地对 9 个语音数据集的语音数据集文档 (VDD) 进行采样。然后，我们的研究结果使用 MLP 角色和权衡的视角对这两种方法进行了三角测量。我们发现当前的 VDD 实践还不成熟、不充分且不相称。语音数据集的特征以碎片化、不相交的方式进行编码，这通常不能满足 MLP 的需求。此外，它们不能轻易进行比较，这给从业者减少偏见的努力带来了障碍。然后，我们讨论这些发现对语音数据和语音技术中的偏见实践的影响。最后，我们制定了一个未来工作计划来解决这些发现——也就是说，我们如何“纠正文档”。 本文通过实证研究分析语音数据集文档实践，揭示其不足并提出改进方向以减少语音技术中的偏见。 Kathy Reid,Elizabeth T. Williams cs.HC,cs.CY,cs.SD,eess.AS

authors_text: Junjiao Tian,Xiaoliang Dai,Chih-Yao Ma,Zecheng He,Yen-Cheng Liu,Zsolt Kira
title: Trainable Projected Gradient Method for Robust Fine-tuning
title_zh: 用于鲁棒微调的可训练投影梯度方法
abstract: Recent studies on transfer learning have shown that selectively fine-tuning a subset of layers or customizing different learning rates for each layer can greatly improve robustness to out-of-distribution (OOD) data and retain generalization capability in the pre-trained models. However, most of these methods employ manually crafted heuristics or expensive hyper-parameter searches, which prevent them from scaling up to large datasets and neural networks. To solve this problem, we propose Trainable Projected Gradient Method (TPGM) to automatically learn the constraint imposed for each layer for a fine-grained fine-tuning regularization. This is motivated by formulating fine-tuning as a bi-level constrained optimization problem. Specifically, TPGM maintains a set of projection radii, i.e., distance constraints between the fine-tuned model and the pre-trained model, for each layer, and enforces them through weight projections. To learn the constraints, we propose a bi-level optimization to automatically learn the best set of projection radii in an end-to-end manner. Theoretically, we show that the bi-level optimization formulation could explain the regularization capability of TPGM. Empirically, with little hyper-parameter search cost, TPGM outperforms existing fine-tuning methods in OOD performance while matching the best in-distribution (ID) performance. For example, when fine-tuned on DomainNet-Real and ImageNet, compared to vanilla fine-tuning, TPGM shows $22\%$ and $10\%$ relative OOD improvement respectively on their sketch counterparts. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/PotatoTian/TPGM}.
summary: Recent studies on transfer learning have shown that selectively fine-tuning a subset of layers or customizing different learning rates for each layer can greatly improve robustness to out-of-distribution (OOD) data and retain generalization capability in the pre-trained models. However, most of these methods employ manually crafted heuristics or expensive hyper-parameter searches, which prevent them from scaling up to large datasets and neural networks. To solve this problem, we propose Trainable Projected Gradient Method (TPGM) to automatically learn the constraint imposed for each layer for a fine-grained fine-tuning regularization. This is motivated by formulating fine-tuning as a bi-level constrained optimization problem. Specifically, TPGM maintains a set of projection radii, i.e., distance constraints between the fine-tuned model and the pre-trained model, for each layer, and enforces them through weight projections. To learn the constraints, we propose a bi-level optimization to automatically learn the best set of projection radii in an end-to-end manner. Theoretically, we show that the bi-level optimization formulation could explain the regularization capability of TPGM. Empirically, with little hyper-parameter search cost, TPGM outperforms existing fine-tuning methods in OOD performance while matching the best in-distribution (ID) performance. For example, when fine-tuned on DomainNet-Real and ImageNet, compared to vanilla fine-tuning, TPGM shows $22\%$ and $10\%$ relative OOD improvement respectively on their sketch counterparts. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/PotatoTian/TPGM}.
abstract_zh: 最近关于迁移学习的研究表明，有选择地微调层的子集或为每个层定制不同的学习率可以极大地提高对分布外（OOD）数据的鲁棒性，并保留预训练模型的泛化能力。然而，这些方法大多数都采用手动设计的启发式方法或昂贵的超参数搜索，这阻碍了它们扩展到大型数据集和神经网络。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了可训练投影梯度法（TPGM）来自动学习对每一层施加的约束，以进行细粒度的微调正则化。这是通过将微调公式化为双层约束优化问题来激发的。具体来说，TPGM 为每一层维护一组投影半径，即微调模型和预训练模型之间的距离约束，并通过权重投影强制它们。为了学习约束，我们提出了一种双层优化，以端到端的方式自动学习最佳的投影半径集。从理论上讲，我们表明双层优化公式可以解释 TPGM 的正则化能力。根据经验，在超参数搜索成本很小的情况下，TPGM 在 OOD 性能方面优于现有的微调方法，同时匹配最佳的分布内 (ID) 性能。例如，当在 DomainNet-Real 和 ImageNet 上进行微调时，与普通微调相比，TPGM 在其草图对应项上分别显示了 $22\%$ 和 $10\%$ 相对 OOD 改进。代码可在 \url{https://github.com/PotatoTian/TPGM} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种可训练投影梯度方法（TPGM），用于在微调预训练模型时自动学习每层的约束条件，以提高对分布外数据的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有微调方法依赖手动设计的启发式规则或耗时的超参数搜索，难以扩展到大规模数据集和神经网络，因此需要一种自动学习约束条件的高效方法。
AI.method: 将微调建模为双层约束优化问题，通过可训练的投影半径（层间距离约束）和端到端的双层优化学习最佳约束，利用权重投影实现正则化。
AI.result: 在DomainNet-Real和ImageNet数据集上，TPGM相比传统微调方法分别在分布外任务上提升了22%和10%的性能，且无需大量超参数调优。
AI.conclusion: TPGM通过双层优化自动学习微调约束，在保持分布内性能的同时显著提升分布外鲁棒性，为大规模模型微调提供了高效解决方案。

authors_text: Giovanni Amendola,Marco Manna,Aldo Ricioppo
title: Characterizing Nexus of Similarity within Knowledge Bases: A Logic-based Framework and its Computational Complexity Aspects
title_zh: 表征知识库中相似性的联系：基于逻辑的框架及其计算复杂性方面
abstract: Similarities between entities occur frequently in many real-world scenarios. For over a century, researchers in different fields have proposed a range of approaches to measure the similarity between entities. More recently, inspired by "Google Sets", significant academic and commercial efforts have been devoted to expanding a given set of entities with similar ones. As a result, existing approaches nowadays are able to take into account properties shared by entities, hereinafter called nexus of similarity. Accordingly, machines are largely able to deal with both similarity measures and set expansions. To the best of our knowledge, however, there is no way to characterize nexus of similarity between entities, namely identifying such nexus in a formal and comprehensive way so that they are both machine- and human-readable; moreover, there is a lack of consensus on evaluating existing approaches for weakly similar entities. As a first step towards filling these gaps, we aim to complement existing literature by developing a novel logic-based framework to formally and automatically characterize nexus of similarity between tuples of entities within a knowledge base. Furthermore, we analyze computational complexity aspects of this framework.
summary: Similarities between entities occur frequently in many real-world scenarios. For over a century, researchers in different fields have proposed a range of approaches to measure the similarity between entities. More recently, inspired by "Google Sets", significant academic and commercial efforts have been devoted to expanding a given set of entities with similar ones. As a result, existing approaches nowadays are able to take into account properties shared by entities, hereinafter called nexus of similarity. Accordingly, machines are largely able to deal with both similarity measures and set expansions. To the best of our knowledge, however, there is no way to characterize nexus of similarity between entities, namely identifying such nexus in a formal and comprehensive way so that they are both machine- and human-readable; moreover, there is a lack of consensus on evaluating existing approaches for weakly similar entities. As a first step towards filling these gaps, we aim to complement existing literature by developing a novel logic-based framework to formally and automatically characterize nexus of similarity between tuples of entities within a knowledge base. Furthermore, we analyze computational complexity aspects of this framework.
abstract_zh: 在许多现实场景中，实体之间的相似性经常出现。一个多世纪以来，不同领域的研究人员提出了一系列测量实体之间相似性的方法。最近，受“Google Sets”的启发，大量的学术和商业努力致力于扩展具有相似实体的给定实体集。因此，当今的现有方法能够考虑实体共享的属性，下文中称为相似性关系。因此，机器很大程度上能够处理相似性度量和集合扩展。然而，据我们所知，没有办法描述实体之间相似性的联系，即以正式和全面的方式识别这种联系，以便它们既是机器可读的又是人类可读的；此外，对于评估弱相似实体的现有方法缺乏共识。作为填补这些空白的第一步，我们的目标是通过开发一种新颖的基于逻辑的框架来补充现有文献，以正式且自动地表征知识库中实体元组之间的相似性关系。此外，我们分析了该框架的计算复杂性方面。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于逻辑的框架，用于在知识库中形式化表征实体间的相似性关联，并分析其计算复杂性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以形式化表征实体间的相似性关联，且缺乏对弱相似实体的评估标准，导致机器与人类难以统一理解。
AI.method: 开发基于逻辑的框架，通过形式化规则自动识别实体元组间的相似性关联，并分析该框架的计算复杂性。
AI.result: 构建了首个形式化表征相似性关联的逻辑框架，并明确了其计算复杂性边界。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为相似性关联提供了机器与人类可读的形式化描述，同时揭示了相关计算复杂性挑战，为后续评估方法奠定了基础。

result: 构建了首个形式化表征相似性关联的逻辑框架，并明确了其计算复杂性边界。
conclusion: 该框架为相似性关联提供了机器与人类可读的形式化描述，同时揭示了相关计算复杂性挑战，为后续评估方法奠定了基础。
search_text: Characterizing Nexus of Similarity within Knowledge Bases: A Logic-based Framework and its Computational Complexity Aspects 表征知识库中相似性的联系：基于逻辑的框架及其计算复杂性方面 Similarities between entities occur frequently in many real-world scenarios. For over a century, researchers in different fields have proposed a range of approaches to measure the similarity between entities. More recently, inspired by "Google Sets", significant academic and commercial efforts have been devoted to expanding a given set of entities with similar ones. As a result, existing approaches nowadays are able to take into account properties shared by entities, hereinafter called nexus of similarity. Accordingly, machines are largely able to deal with both similarity measures and set expansions. To the best of our knowledge, however, there is no way to characterize nexus of similarity between entities, namely identifying such nexus in a formal and comprehensive way so that they are both machine- and human-readable; moreover, there is a lack of consensus on evaluating existing approaches for weakly similar entities. As a first step towards filling these gaps, we aim to complement existing literature by developing a novel logic-based framework to formally and automatically characterize nexus of similarity between tuples of entities within a knowledge base. Furthermore, we analyze computational complexity aspects of this framework. 在许多现实场景中，实体之间的相似性经常出现。一个多世纪以来，不同领域的研究人员提出了一系列测量实体之间相似性的方法。最近，受“Google Sets”的启发，大量的学术和商业努力致力于扩展具有相似实体的给定实体集。因此，当今的现有方法能够考虑实体共享的属性，下文中称为相似性关系。因此，机器很大程度上能够处理相似性度量和集合扩展。然而，据我们所知，没有办法描述实体之间相似性的联系，即以正式和全面的方式识别这种联系，以便它们既是机器可读的又是人类可读的；此外，对于评估弱相似实体的现有方法缺乏共识。作为填补这些空白的第一步，我们的目标是通过开发一种新颖的基于逻辑的框架来补充现有文献，以正式且自动地表征知识库中实体元组之间的相似性关系。此外，我们分析了该框架的计算复杂性方面。 本文提出一种基于逻辑的框架，用于在知识库中形式化表征实体间的相似性关联，并分析其计算复杂性。 Giovanni Amendola,Marco Manna,Aldo Ricioppo cs.AI

authors_text: Junyuan Deng,Xieyuanli Chen,Songpengcheng Xia,Zhen Sun,Guoqing Liu,Wenxian Yu,Ling Pei
title: NeRF-LOAM: Neural Implicit Representation for Large-Scale Incremental LiDAR Odometry and Mapping
title_zh: NeRF-LOAM：大规模增量 LiDAR 里程计和测绘的神经隐式表示
abstract: Simultaneously odometry and mapping using LiDAR data is an important task for mobile systems to achieve full autonomy in large-scale environments. However, most existing LiDAR-based methods prioritize tracking quality over reconstruction quality. Although the recently developed neural radiance fields (NeRF) have shown promising advances in implicit reconstruction for indoor environments, the problem of simultaneous odometry and mapping for large-scale scenarios using incremental LiDAR data remains unexplored. To bridge this gap, in this paper, we propose a novel NeRF-based LiDAR odometry and mapping approach, NeRF-LOAM, consisting of three modules neural odometry, neural mapping, and mesh reconstruction. All these modules utilize our proposed neural signed distance function, which separates LiDAR points into ground and non-ground points to reduce Z-axis drift, optimizes odometry and voxel embeddings concurrently, and in the end generates dense smooth mesh maps of the environment. Moreover, this joint optimization allows our NeRF-LOAM to be pre-trained free and exhibit strong generalization abilities when applied to different environments. Extensive evaluations on three publicly available datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art odometry and mapping performance, as well as a strong generalization in large-scale environments utilizing LiDAR data. Furthermore, we perform multiple ablation studies to validate the effectiveness of our network design. The implementation of our approach will be made available at https://github.com/JunyuanDeng/NeRF-LOAM.
summary: Simultaneously odometry and mapping using LiDAR data is an important task for mobile systems to achieve full autonomy in large-scale environments. However, most existing LiDAR-based methods prioritize tracking quality over reconstruction quality. Although the recently developed neural radiance fields (NeRF) have shown promising advances in implicit reconstruction for indoor environments, the problem of simultaneous odometry and mapping for large-scale scenarios using incremental LiDAR data remains unexplored. To bridge this gap, in this paper, we propose a novel NeRF-based LiDAR odometry and mapping approach, NeRF-LOAM, consisting of three modules neural odometry, neural mapping, and mesh reconstruction. All these modules utilize our proposed neural signed distance function, which separates LiDAR points into ground and non-ground points to reduce Z-axis drift, optimizes odometry and voxel embeddings concurrently, and in the end generates dense smooth mesh maps of the environment. Moreover, this joint optimization allows our NeRF-LOAM to be pre-trained free and exhibit strong generalization abilities when applied to different environments. Extensive evaluations on three publicly available datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art odometry and mapping performance, as well as a strong generalization in large-scale environments utilizing LiDAR data. Furthermore, we perform multiple ablation studies to validate the effectiveness of our network design. The implementation of our approach will be made available at https://github.com/JunyuanDeng/NeRF-LOAM.
abstract_zh: 使用激光雷达数据同时进行里程计和测绘是移动系统在大规模环境中实现完全自主的一项重要任务。然而，大多数现有的基于激光雷达的方法优先考虑跟踪质量而不是重建质量。尽管最近开发的神经辐射场（NeRF）在室内环境的隐式重建方面显示出了有希望的进展，但使用增量激光雷达数据进行大规模场景的同步测距和绘图问题仍未得到探索。为了弥补这一差距，在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于 NeRF 的 LiDAR 里程计和建图方法 NeRF-LOAM，由神经里程计、神经建图和网格重建三个模块组成。所有这些模块都利用我们提出的神经符号距离函数，该函数将 LiDAR 点分为地面点和非地面点，以减少 Z 轴漂移，同时优化里程计和体素嵌入，并最终生成环境的密集平滑网格图。此外，这种联合优化使得我们的 NeRF-LOAM 可以免预训练，并在应用于不同环境时表现出强大的泛化能力。对三个公开可用数据集的广泛评估表明，我们的方法实现了最先进的里程计和地图性能，以及在利用 LiDAR 数据的大规模环境中的强大泛化能力。此外，我们进行了多项消融研究来验证我们网络设计的有效性。我们的方法的实施将在 https://github.com/JunyuanDeng/NeRF-LOAM 上提供。

AI.tldr: 本文提出NeRF-LOAM方法，利用神经隐式表示实现大规模增量激光雷达里程计与建图。
AI.motivation: 现有激光雷达方法侧重跟踪质量而忽视重建质量，且神经辐射场（NeRF）在大规模场景下的增量激光雷达同步定位与建图尚未被探索。
AI.method: 设计包含神经里程计、神经建图和网格重建三个模块的NeRF-LOAM框架，通过神经符号距离函数分离地物点以减少Z轴漂移，并联合优化里程计与体素嵌入。
AI.result: 在三个公开数据集上达到最先进的里程计与建图性能，展现强环境泛化能力，并通过消融实验验证网络设计有效性。
AI.conclusion: NeRF-LOAM通过神经隐式表示与激光雷达数据融合，在大规模场景中实现了高精度、强泛化的同步定位与建图。

authors_text: Mandy Keck,Samuel Huron,Georgia Panagiotidou,Christina Stoiber,Fateme Rajabiyazdi,Charles Perin,Jonathan C. Roberts,Benjamin Bach
title: EduVis: Workshop on Visualization Education, Literacy, and Activities
title_zh: EduVis：可视化教育、素养和活动研讨会
comment: 5 pages, no figures, accepted workshop for IEEE VIS 2023
comments: 5 pages, no figures, accepted workshop for IEEE VIS 2023
abstract: This workshop focuses on visualization education, literacy, and activities. It aims to streamline previous efforts and initiatives of the visualization community to provide a format for education and engagement practices in visualization. It intends to bring together junior and senior scholars to share research and experience and to discuss novel activities, teaching methods, and research challenges. The workshop aims to serve as a platform for interdisciplinary researchers within and beyond the visualization community such as education, learning analytics, science communication, psychology, or people from adjacent fields such as data science, AI, and HCI. It will include presentations of research papers and practical reports, as well as hands-on activities. In addition, the workshop will allow participants to discuss challenges they face in data visualization education and sketch a research agenda of visualization education, literacy, and activities.
summary: This workshop focuses on visualization education, literacy, and activities. It aims to streamline previous efforts and initiatives of the visualization community to provide a format for education and engagement practices in visualization. It intends to bring together junior and senior scholars to share research and experience and to discuss novel activities, teaching methods, and research challenges. The workshop aims to serve as a platform for interdisciplinary researchers within and beyond the visualization community such as education, learning analytics, science communication, psychology, or people from adjacent fields such as data science, AI, and HCI. It will include presentations of research papers and practical reports, as well as hands-on activities. In addition, the workshop will allow participants to discuss challenges they face in data visualization education and sketch a research agenda of visualization education, literacy, and activities.
abstract_zh: 本次研讨会的重点是可视化教育、读写能力和活动。它旨在简化可视化社区之前的努力和举措，为可视化的教育和参与实践提供一种格式。它旨在将初级和高级学者聚集在一起，分享研究和经验，并讨论新颖的活动、教学方法和研究挑战。该研讨会旨在为可视化社区内外的跨学科研究人员（例如教育、学习分析、科学传播、心理学）或来自数据科学、人工智能和人机交互等邻近领域的人们提供一个平台。它将包括研究论文和实践报告的介绍以及实践活动。此外，研讨会还将允许参与者讨论他们在数据可视化教育中面临的挑战，并草拟可视化教育、素养和活动的研究议程。

AI.tldr: 组织研讨会，整合可视化教育的现有努力，促进跨学科交流，分享教学方法和研究挑战。
AI.motivation: 推动可视化教育的发展，促进跨学科合作，解决教育中的实际问题，并制定未来的研究方向。
AI.method: 通过研讨会形式，结合论文展示、实践报告、动手活动及小组讨论，促进知识分享和合作。
AI.result: 收集了多种可视化教育方法和实践案例，明确了当前挑战，并初步形成未来研究方向。
AI.conclusion: 跨学科研讨会有效促进可视化教育的交流与创新，未来需持续推动教育实践与研究的结合。

authors_text: Marcella Astrid,Muhammad Zaigham Zaheer,Seung-Ik Lee
title: PseudoBound: Limiting the anomaly reconstruction capability of one-class classifiers using pseudo anomalies
title_zh: PseudoBound：使用伪异常限制一类分类器的异常重建能力
comment: ef:Marcella Astrid, Muhammad Zaigham Zaheer, and Seung-Ik Lee. "PseudoBound: Limiting the Anomaly Reconstruction Capability of One-Class Classifiers Using Pseudo Anomalies". In: Neurocomputing 534 (May 14, 2023), pp. 147-160
comments: ef:Marcella Astrid, Muhammad Zaigham Zaheer, and Seung-Ik Lee. "PseudoBound: Limiting the Anomaly Reconstruction Capability of One-Class Classifiers Using Pseudo Anomalies". In: Neurocomputing 534 (May 14, 2023), pp. 147-160
abstract: Due to the rarity of anomalous events, video anomaly detection is typically approached as one-class classification (OCC) problem. Typically in OCC, an autoencoder (AE) is trained to reconstruct the normal only training data with the expectation that, in test time, it can poorly reconstruct the anomalous data. However, previous studies have shown that, even trained with only normal data, AEs can often reconstruct anomalous data as well, resulting in a decreased performance. To mitigate this problem, we propose to limit the anomaly reconstruction capability of AEs by incorporating pseudo anomalies during the training of an AE. Extensive experiments using five types of pseudo anomalies show the robustness of our training mechanism towards any kind of pseudo anomaly. Moreover, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed pseudo anomaly based training approach against several existing state-ofthe-art (SOTA) methods on three benchmark video anomaly datasets, outperforming all the other reconstruction-based approaches in two datasets and showing the second best performance in the other dataset.
summary: Due to the rarity of anomalous events, video anomaly detection is typically approached as one-class classification (OCC) problem. Typically in OCC, an autoencoder (AE) is trained to reconstruct the normal only training data with the expectation that, in test time, it can poorly reconstruct the anomalous data. However, previous studies have shown that, even trained with only normal data, AEs can often reconstruct anomalous data as well, resulting in a decreased performance. To mitigate this problem, we propose to limit the anomaly reconstruction capability of AEs by incorporating pseudo anomalies during the training of an AE. Extensive experiments using five types of pseudo anomalies show the robustness of our training mechanism towards any kind of pseudo anomaly. Moreover, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed pseudo anomaly based training approach against several existing state-ofthe-art (SOTA) methods on three benchmark video anomaly datasets, outperforming all the other reconstruction-based approaches in two datasets and showing the second best performance in the other dataset.
abstract_zh: 由于异常事件的罕见性，视频异常检测通常被视为一类分类（OCC）问题。通常在 OCC 中，自动编码器 (AE) 被训练来重建仅正常的训练数据，并期望在测试时间内它无法重建异常数据。然而，之前的研究表明，即使仅使用正常数据进行训练，AE 通常也可以重建异常数据，从而导致性能下降。为了缓解这个问题，我们建议通过在 AE 的训练过程中加入伪异常来限制 AE 的异常重建能力。使用五种类型的伪异常进行的广泛实验表明，我们的训练机制对任何类型的伪异常都有鲁棒性。此外，我们在三个基准视频异常数据集上证明了我们提出的基于伪异常的训练方法相对于几种现有最先进（SOTA）方法的有效性，在两个数据集中优于所有其他基于重建的方法，并在另一个数据集中显示出第二好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PseudoBound方法，通过引入伪异常限制自编码器的异常重构能力，提升视频异常检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有基于自编码器的一类分类方法在仅用正常数据训练时，仍可能重构异常数据，导致检测性能下降。
AI.method: 在自编码器训练过程中注入伪异常数据，通过五种伪异常类型增强模型对异常的鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在三个基准视频异常数据集上，该方法在两个数据集上优于现有最优方法，在另一数据集上取得第二佳成绩。
AI.conclusion: 伪异常训练机制能有效限制异常重构能力，提升视频异常检测效果且对伪异常类型具有鲁棒性。

authors_text: Srikar Yellapragada,Zhenghong Li,Kevin Bhadresh Doshi,Purva Makarand Mhasakar,Heng Fan,Jie Wei,Erik Blasch,Bin Zhang,Haibin Ling
title: CCTV-Gun: Benchmarking Handgun Detection in CCTV Images
title_zh: CCTV-Gun：闭路电视图像中手枪检测的基准测试
abstract: Gun violence is a critical security problem, and it is imperative for the computer vision community to develop effective gun detection algorithms for real-world scenarios, particularly in Closed Circuit Television (CCTV) surveillance data. Despite significant progress in visual object detection, detecting guns in real-world CCTV images remains a challenging and under-explored task. Firearms, especially handguns, are typically very small in size, non-salient in appearance, and often severely occluded or indistinguishable from other small objects. Additionally, the lack of principled benchmarks and difficulty collecting relevant datasets further hinder algorithmic development. In this paper, we present a meticulously crafted and annotated benchmark, called \textbf{CCTV-Gun}, which addresses the challenges of detecting handguns in real-world CCTV images. Our contribution is three-fold. Firstly, we carefully select and analyze real-world CCTV images from three datasets, manually annotate handguns and their holders, and assign each image with relevant challenge factors such as blur and occlusion. Secondly, we propose a new cross-dataset evaluation protocol in addition to the standard intra-dataset protocol, which is vital for gun detection in practical settings. Finally, we comprehensively evaluate both classical and state-of-the-art object detection algorithms, providing an in-depth analysis of their generalizing abilities. The benchmark will facilitate further research and development on this topic and ultimately enhance security. Code, annotations, and trained models are available at https://github.com/srikarym/CCTV-Gun.
summary: Gun violence is a critical security problem, and it is imperative for the computer vision community to develop effective gun detection algorithms for real-world scenarios, particularly in Closed Circuit Television (CCTV) surveillance data. Despite significant progress in visual object detection, detecting guns in real-world CCTV images remains a challenging and under-explored task. Firearms, especially handguns, are typically very small in size, non-salient in appearance, and often severely occluded or indistinguishable from other small objects. Additionally, the lack of principled benchmarks and difficulty collecting relevant datasets further hinder algorithmic development. In this paper, we present a meticulously crafted and annotated benchmark, called \textbf{CCTV-Gun}, which addresses the challenges of detecting handguns in real-world CCTV images. Our contribution is three-fold. Firstly, we carefully select and analyze real-world CCTV images from three datasets, manually annotate handguns and their holders, and assign each image with relevant challenge factors such as blur and occlusion. Secondly, we propose a new cross-dataset evaluation protocol in addition to the standard intra-dataset protocol, which is vital for gun detection in practical settings. Finally, we comprehensively evaluate both classical and state-of-the-art object detection algorithms, providing an in-depth analysis of their generalizing abilities. The benchmark will facilitate further research and development on this topic and ultimately enhance security. Code, annotations, and trained models are available at https://github.com/srikarym/CCTV-Gun.
abstract_zh: 枪支暴力是一个关键的安全问题，计算机视觉社区必须为现实场景开发有效的枪支检测算法，特别是在闭路电视（CCTV）监控数据中。尽管视觉对象检测取得了重大进展，但在现实世界的闭路电视图像中检测枪支仍然是一项具有挑战性且尚未充分探索的任务。枪支，特别是手枪，通常尺寸非常小，外观不显眼，并且经常被严重遮挡或与其他小物体无法区分。此外，缺乏原则基准和收集相关数据集的困难进一步阻碍了算法的开发。在本文中，我们提出了一个精心设计和注释的基准，称为 \textbf{CCTV-Gun}，它解决了在现实世界的闭路电视图像中检测手枪的挑战。我们的贡献是三方面的。首先，我们从三个数据集中仔细选择和分析真实世界的闭路电视图像，手动注释手枪及其持有者，并为每个图像分配相关的挑战因素，例如模糊和遮挡。其次，除了标准的数据集内协议之外，我们还提出了一种新的跨数据集评估协议，这对于实际环境中的枪支检测至关重要。最后，我们全面评估经典和最先进的目标检测算法，对其泛化能力进行深入分析。该基准将促进该主题的进一步研究和开发，并最终增强安全性。代码、注释和训练模型可在 https://github.com/srikarym/CCTV-Gun 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CCTV-Gun基准，用于评估和推动在CCTV图像中检测手枪的研究。
AI.motivation: 真实场景中CCTV图像的手枪检测面临小尺寸、遮挡、缺乏基准数据集等挑战，阻碍算法发展。
AI.method: 构建包含标注手枪及挑战因素的CCTV-Gun数据集，设计跨数据集评估协议，并测试经典与先进检测算法。
AI.result: 提供了首个针对CCTV手枪检测的基准，分析了不同算法的泛化能力及性能差异。
AI.conclusion: CCTV-Gun基准将促进该领域研究，提升实际安防中的枪支检测能力。

authors_text: Yinsong Wang,Huaqi Qiu,Chen Qin
title: Conditional Deformable Image Registration with Spatially-Variant and Adaptive Regularization
title_zh: 具有空间变化和自适应正则化的条件可变形图像配准
comment: 5 pages, 5 figures, 1 tables. The paper is accepted by the IEEE International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI) 2023
comments: 5 pages, 5 figures, 1 tables. The paper is accepted by the IEEE International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI) 2023
abstract: Deep learning-based image registration approaches have shown competitive performance and run-time advantages compared to conventional image registration methods. However, existing learning-based approaches mostly require to train separate models with respect to different regularization hyperparameters for manual hyperparameter searching and often do not allow spatially-variant regularization. In this work, we propose a learning-based registration approach based on a novel conditional spatially adaptive instance normalization (CSAIN) to address these challenges. The proposed method introduces a spatially-variant regularization and learns its effect of achieving spatially-adaptive regularization by conditioning the registration network on the hyperparameter matrix via CSAIN. This allows varying of spatially adaptive regularization at inference to obtain multiple plausible deformations with a single pre-trained model. Additionally, the proposed method enables automatic hyperparameter optimization to avoid manual hyperparameter searching. Experiments show that our proposed method outperforms the baseline approaches while achieving spatially-variant and adaptive regularization.
summary: Deep learning-based image registration approaches have shown competitive performance and run-time advantages compared to conventional image registration methods. However, existing learning-based approaches mostly require to train separate models with respect to different regularization hyperparameters for manual hyperparameter searching and often do not allow spatially-variant regularization. In this work, we propose a learning-based registration approach based on a novel conditional spatially adaptive instance normalization (CSAIN) to address these challenges. The proposed method introduces a spatially-variant regularization and learns its effect of achieving spatially-adaptive regularization by conditioning the registration network on the hyperparameter matrix via CSAIN. This allows varying of spatially adaptive regularization at inference to obtain multiple plausible deformations with a single pre-trained model. Additionally, the proposed method enables automatic hyperparameter optimization to avoid manual hyperparameter searching. Experiments show that our proposed method outperforms the baseline approaches while achieving spatially-variant and adaptive regularization.
abstract_zh: 与传统的图像配准方法相比，基于深度学习的图像配准方法显示出有竞争力的性能和运行时间优势。然而，现有的基于学习的方法大多需要针对不同的正则化超参数训练单独的模型以进行手动超参数搜索，并且通常不允许空间变化的正则化。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种基于学习的配准方法，该方法基于新颖的条件空间自适应实例归一化（CSAIN）来应对这些挑战。该方法引入了空间变化正则化，并通过 CSAIN 在超参数矩阵上调节配准网络来了解其实现空间自适应正则化的效果。这允许在推理时改变空间自适应正则化，以使用单个预训练模型获得多个合理的变形。此外，所提出的方法能够自动进行超参数优化，以避免手动超参数搜索。实验表明，我们提出的方法优于基线方法，同时实现了空间变化和自适应正则化。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于条件空间自适应实例归一化的图像配准方法，解决现有方法在正则化超参数调整和空间可变性方面的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习图像配准方法需手动调整超参数并训练多个模型，且无法实现空间可变正则化，限制了其灵活性和效率。
AI.method: 通过引入条件空间自适应实例归一化（CSAIN），将注册网络条件化于超参数矩阵，实现空间自适应正则化与自动超参数优化。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在保持空间可变正则化的同时优于基线方法，且无需手动调整超参数。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了图像配准中的正则化挑战，具有实际应用价值。

ai_content.result: 实验表明该方法在保持空间可变正则化的同时优于基线方法，且无需手动调整超参数。
ai_content.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了图像配准中的正则化挑战，具有实际应用价值。

search_text: Conditional Deformable Image Registration with Spatially-Variant and Adaptive Regularization 具有空间变化和自适应正则化的条件可变形图像配准 Deep learning-based image registration approaches have shown competitive performance and run-time advantages compared to conventional image registration methods. However, existing learning-based approaches mostly require to train separate models with respect to different regularization hyperparameters for manual hyperparameter searching and often do not allow spatially-variant regularization. In this work, we propose a learning-based registration approach based on a novel conditional spatially adaptive instance normalization (CSAIN) to address these challenges. The proposed method introduces a spatially-variant regularization and learns its effect of achieving spatially-adaptive regularization by conditioning the registration network on the hyperparameter matrix via CSAIN. This allows varying of spatially adaptive regularization at inference to obtain multiple plausible deformations with a single pre-trained model. Additionally, the proposed method enables automatic hyperparameter optimization to avoid manual hyperparameter searching. Experiments show that our proposed method outperforms the baseline approaches while achieving spatially-variant and adaptive regularization. 与传统的图像配准方法相比，基于深度学习的图像配准方法显示出有竞争力的性能和运行时间优势。然而，现有的基于学习的方法大多需要针对不同的正则化超参数训练单独的模型以进行手动超参数搜索，并且通常不允许空间变化的正则化。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种基于学习的配准方法，该方法基于新颖的条件空间自适应实例归一化（CSAIN）来应对这些挑战。该方法引入了空间变化正则化，并通过 CSAIN 在超参数矩阵上调节配准网络来了解其实现空间自适应正则化的效果。这允许在推理时改变空间自适应正则化，以使用单个预训练模型获得多个合理的变形。此外，所提出的方法能够自动进行超参数优化，以避免手动超参数搜索。实验表明，我们提出的方法优于基线方法，同时实现了空间变化和自适应正则化。 本文提出一种基于条件空间自适应实例归一化的图像配准方法，解决现有方法在正则化超参数调整和空间可变性方面的不足。 Yinsong Wang,Huaqi Qiu,Chen Qin eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Weizhe Lin,Zhilin Wang,Bill Byrne
title: FVQA 2.0: Introducing Adversarial Samples into Fact-based Visual Question Answering
title_zh: FVQA 2.0：将对抗样本引入基于事实的视觉问答
abstract: The widely used Fact-based Visual Question Answering (FVQA) dataset contains visually-grounded questions that require information retrieval using common sense knowledge graphs to answer. It has been observed that the original dataset is highly imbalanced and concentrated on a small portion of its associated knowledge graph. We introduce FVQA 2.0 which contains adversarial variants of test questions to address this imbalance. We show that systems trained with the original FVQA train sets can be vulnerable to adversarial samples and we demonstrate an augmentation scheme to reduce this vulnerability without human annotations.
summary: The widely used Fact-based Visual Question Answering (FVQA) dataset contains visually-grounded questions that require information retrieval using common sense knowledge graphs to answer. It has been observed that the original dataset is highly imbalanced and concentrated on a small portion of its associated knowledge graph. We introduce FVQA 2.0 which contains adversarial variants of test questions to address this imbalance. We show that systems trained with the original FVQA train sets can be vulnerable to adversarial samples and we demonstrate an augmentation scheme to reduce this vulnerability without human annotations.
abstract_zh: 广泛使用的基于事实的视觉问答（FVQA）数据集包含基于视觉的问题，需要使用常识知识图进行信息检索来回答。据观察，原始数据集高度不平衡，并且集中于其相关知识图谱的一小部分。我们引入了 FVQA 2.0，其中包含测试问题的对抗性变体来解决这种不平衡问题。我们表明，使用原始 FVQA 训练集训练的系统可能容易受到对抗性样本的影响，并且我们演示了一种增强方案，可以在没有人工注释的情况下减少这种漏洞。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FVQA 2.0数据集，通过引入对抗样本解决事实性视觉问答数据集的不平衡问题。
AI.motivation: 原始FVQA数据集存在高度不平衡问题，过度集中在知识图谱的小部分区域，导致模型易受对抗样本攻击。
AI.method: 通过生成对抗样本扩展测试问题，并设计无需人工标注的增强方案提升模型鲁棒性。
AI.result: 实验表明原始训练模型对对抗样本脆弱，而采用增强方案后模型的抗干扰能力显著提升。
AI.conclusion: FVQA 2.0通过对抗样本注入和自动增强方法，有效改善了数据集平衡性并提升了模型的对抗鲁棒性。

authors_text: Chaofan Ma,Qisen Xu,Xiangfeng Wang,Bo Jin,Xiaoyun Zhang,Yanfeng Wang,Ya Zhang
title: Boundary-aware Supervoxel-level Iteratively Refined Interactive 3D Image Segmentation with Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 通过多智能体强化学习进行边界感知超体素级迭代细化交互式 3D 图像分割
comment: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
comments: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Interactive segmentation has recently been explored to effectively and efficiently harvest high-quality segmentation masks by iteratively incorporating user hints. While iterative in nature, most existing interactive segmentation methods tend to ignore the dynamics of successive interactions and take each interaction independently. We here propose to model iterative interactive image segmentation with a Markov decision process (MDP) and solve it with reinforcement learning (RL) where each voxel is treated as an agent. Considering the large exploration space for voxel-wise prediction and the dependence among neighboring voxels for the segmentation tasks, multi-agent reinforcement learning is adopted, where the voxel-level policy is shared among agents. Considering that boundary voxels are more important for segmentation, we further introduce a boundary-aware reward, which consists of a global reward in the form of relative cross-entropy gain, to update the policy in a constrained direction, and a boundary reward in the form of relative weight, to emphasize the correctness of boundary predictions. To combine the advantages of different types of interactions, i.e., simple and efficient for point-clicking, and stable and robust for scribbles, we propose a supervoxel-clicking based interaction design. Experimental results on four benchmark datasets have shown that the proposed method significantly outperforms the state-of-the-arts, with the advantage of fewer interactions, higher accuracy, and enhanced robustness.
summary: Interactive segmentation has recently been explored to effectively and efficiently harvest high-quality segmentation masks by iteratively incorporating user hints. While iterative in nature, most existing interactive segmentation methods tend to ignore the dynamics of successive interactions and take each interaction independently. We here propose to model iterative interactive image segmentation with a Markov decision process (MDP) and solve it with reinforcement learning (RL) where each voxel is treated as an agent. Considering the large exploration space for voxel-wise prediction and the dependence among neighboring voxels for the segmentation tasks, multi-agent reinforcement learning is adopted, where the voxel-level policy is shared among agents. Considering that boundary voxels are more important for segmentation, we further introduce a boundary-aware reward, which consists of a global reward in the form of relative cross-entropy gain, to update the policy in a constrained direction, and a boundary reward in the form of relative weight, to emphasize the correctness of boundary predictions. To combine the advantages of different types of interactions, i.e., simple and efficient for point-clicking, and stable and robust for scribbles, we propose a supervoxel-clicking based interaction design. Experimental results on four benchmark datasets have shown that the proposed method significantly outperforms the state-of-the-arts, with the advantage of fewer interactions, higher accuracy, and enhanced robustness.
abstract_zh: 最近，人们探索了交互式分割，通过迭代地合并用户提示来有效且高效地收获高质量的分割掩模。虽然本质上是迭代的，但大多数现有的交互分割方法往往忽略连续交互的动态并独立地对待每个交互。我们在这里建议使用马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）对迭代交互式图像分割进行建模，并通过强化学习（RL）来解决它，其中每个体素都被视为一个代理。考虑到体素预测的巨大探索空间以及分割任务中相邻体素之间的依赖性，采用多智能体强化学习，其中体素级策略在智能体之间共享。考虑到边界体素对于分割更重要，我们进一步引入了边界感知奖励，它包括相对交叉熵增益形式的全局奖励，以在约束方向上更新策略，以及相对权重形式的边界奖励，以强调边界预测的正确性。为了结合不同类型交互的优点，即点击简单高效，涂鸦稳定鲁棒，我们提出了一种基于超体素点击的交互设计。在四个基准数据集上的实验结果表明，所提出的方法显着优于最先进的方法，具有交互更少、准确性更高、鲁棒性增强的优点。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多智能体强化学习的边界感知监督体级迭代交互式3D图像分割方法。
AI.motivation: 现有交互式分割方法忽视了连续交互的动态性，且未充分重视边界体素的重要性，导致分割效率和精度不足。
AI.method: 将分割建模为马尔可夫决策过程，采用多智能体强化学习框架，共享体素级策略，并引入边界感知奖励机制与监督体点击交互设计。
AI.result: 在四个基准数据集上显著优于现有方法，具有更少交互次数、更高精度和更强鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过动态建模与边界优化有效提升了交互式3D分割的性能。

authors_text: Chunmeng Liu,Guangyao Li,Yao Shen,Ruiqi Wang
title: MECPformer: Multi-estimations Complementary Patch with CNN-Transformers for Weakly Supervised Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: MECPformer：用于弱监督语义分割的 CNN-Transformers 多重估计补充补丁
abstract: The initial seed based on the convolutional neural network (CNN) for weakly supervised semantic segmentation always highlights the most discriminative regions but fails to identify the global target information. Methods based on transformers have been proposed successively benefiting from the advantage of capturing long-range feature representations. However, we observe a flaw regardless of the gifts based on the transformer. Given a class, the initial seeds generated based on the transformer may invade regions belonging to other classes. Inspired by the mentioned issues, we devise a simple yet effective method with Multi-estimations Complementary Patch (MECP) strategy and Adaptive Conflict Module (ACM), dubbed MECPformer. Given an image, we manipulate it with the MECP strategy at different epochs, and the network mines and deeply fuses the semantic information at different levels. In addition, ACM adaptively removes conflicting pixels and exploits the network self-training capability to mine potential target information. Without bells and whistles, our MECPformer has reached new state-of-the-art 72.0% mIoU on the PASCAL VOC 2012 and 42.4% on MS COCO 2014 dataset. The code is available at https://github.com/ChunmengLiu1/MECPformer.
summary: The initial seed based on the convolutional neural network (CNN) for weakly supervised semantic segmentation always highlights the most discriminative regions but fails to identify the global target information. Methods based on transformers have been proposed successively benefiting from the advantage of capturing long-range feature representations. However, we observe a flaw regardless of the gifts based on the transformer. Given a class, the initial seeds generated based on the transformer may invade regions belonging to other classes. Inspired by the mentioned issues, we devise a simple yet effective method with Multi-estimations Complementary Patch (MECP) strategy and Adaptive Conflict Module (ACM), dubbed MECPformer. Given an image, we manipulate it with the MECP strategy at different epochs, and the network mines and deeply fuses the semantic information at different levels. In addition, ACM adaptively removes conflicting pixels and exploits the network self-training capability to mine potential target information. Without bells and whistles, our MECPformer has reached new state-of-the-art 72.0% mIoU on the PASCAL VOC 2012 and 42.4% on MS COCO 2014 dataset. The code is available at https://github.com/ChunmengLiu1/MECPformer.
abstract_zh: 基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的弱监督语义分割的初始种子总是突出最具辨别力的区域，但无法识别全局目标信息。受益于捕获远程特征表示的优势，基于变压器的方法相继被提出。然而，无论基于变压器的礼物如何，我们都观察到一个缺陷。给定一个类，基于变压器生成的初始种子可能会侵入属于其他类的区域。受上述问题的启发，我们设计了一种简单而有效的方法，采用多重估计补充补丁（MECP）策略和自适应冲突模块（ACM），称为MECPformer。给定一张图像，我们在不同时期用MECP策略对其进行操作，网络挖掘并深度融合不同层次的语义信息。此外，ACM自适应地去除冲突像素并利用网络自训练能力来挖掘潜在的目标信息。没有花哨的东西，我们的 MECPformer 在 PASCAL VOC 2012 上达到了最先进的 72.0% mIoU，在 MS COCO 2014 数据集上达到了 42.4%。代码可在 https://github.com/ChunmengLiu1/MECPformer 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为MECPformer的弱监督语义分割方法，结合CNN和Transformer的优势，以提高分割性能。
AI.motivation: 基于CNN的方法在弱监督语义分割中虽然能突出最具有区分性的区域，但无法捕捉全局目标信息。而基于Transformer的方法虽然能够捕获长距离特征表示，但生成的初始种子可能会侵犯其他类别的区域。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种多估计互补补丁（MECP）策略和自适应冲突模块（ACM），通过在不同阶段对图像进行MECP操作，网络能够挖掘和深度融合不同层次的语义信息。ACM则自适应地去除冲突像素，利用网络的自训练能力挖掘潜在的目标信息。
AI.result: 在PASCAL VOC 2012数据集上，MECPformer达到了72.0%的mIoU，在MS COCO 2014数据集上达到了42.4%的mIoU。
AI.conclusion: MECPformer通过结合CNN和Transformer的优势，有效解决了弱监督语义分割中的问题，取得了当前最先进的性能。

search_text: MECPformer: Multi-estimations Complementary Patch with CNN-Transformers for Weakly Supervised Semantic Segmentation MECPformer：用于弱监督语义分割的 CNN-Transformers 多重估计补充补丁 The initial seed based on the convolutional neural network (CNN) for weakly supervised semantic segmentation always highlights the most discriminative regions but fails to identify the global target information. Methods based on transformers have been proposed successively benefiting from the advantage of capturing long-range feature representations. However, we observe a flaw regardless of the gifts based on the transformer. Given a class, the initial seeds generated based on the transformer may invade regions belonging to other classes. Inspired by the mentioned issues, we devise a simple yet effective method with Multi-estimations Complementary Patch (MECP) strategy and Adaptive Conflict Module (ACM), dubbed MECPformer. Given an image, we manipulate it with the MECP strategy at different epochs, and the network mines and deeply fuses the semantic information at different levels. In addition, ACM adaptively removes conflicting pixels and exploits the network self-training capability to mine potential target information. Without bells and whistles, our MECPformer has reached new state-of-the-art 72.0% mIoU on the PASCAL VOC 2012 and 42.4% on MS COCO 2014 dataset. The code is available at https://github.com/ChunmengLiu1/MECPformer. 基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的弱监督语义分割的初始种子总是突出最具辨别力的区域，但无法识别全局目标信息。受益于捕获远程特征表示的优势，基于变压器的方法相继被提出。然而，无论基于变压器的礼物如何，我们都观察到一个缺陷。给定一个类，基于变压器生成的初始种子可能会侵入属于其他类的区域。受上述问题的启发，我们设计了一种简单而有效的方法，采用多重估计补充补丁（MECP）策略和自适应冲突模块（ACM），称为MECPformer。给定一张图像，我们在不同时期用MECP策略对其进行操作，网络挖掘并深度融合不同层次的语义信息。此外，ACM自适应地去除冲突像素并利用网络自训练能力来挖掘潜在的目标信息。没有花哨的东西，我们的 MECPformer 在 PASCAL VOC 2012 上达到了最先进的 72.0% mIoU，在 MS COCO 2014 数据集上达到了 42.4%。代码可在 https://github.com/ChunmengLiu1/MECPformer 获取。 本文提出了一种名为MECPformer的弱监督语义分割方法，结合CNN和Transformer的优势，以提高分割性能。 Chunmeng Liu,Guangyao Li,Yao Shen,Ruiqi Wang cs.CV

authors_text: Leonie Becker,Tommy Nilsson,Paul Topf Aguiar de Medeiros,Flavie Rometsch
title: Augmented Reality in Service of Human Operations on the Moon: Insights from a Virtual Testbed
title_zh: 增强现实在月球人类操作服务中的应用：来自虚拟测试台的见解
comment: Extended Abstracts of the 2023 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI'23)
comments: Extended Abstracts of the 2023 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI'23)
abstract: Future astronauts living and working on the Moon will face extreme environmental conditions impeding their operational safety and performance. While it has been suggested that Augmented Reality (AR) Head-Up Displays (HUDs) could potentially help mitigate some of these adversities, the applicability of AR in the unique lunar context remains underexplored. To address this limitation, we have produced an accurate representation of the lunar setting in virtual reality (VR) which then formed our testbed for the exploration of prospective operational scenarios with aerospace experts. Herein we present findings based on qualitative reflections made by the first 6 study participants. AR was found instrumental in several use cases, including the support of navigation and risk awareness. Major design challenges were likewise identified, including the importance of redundancy and contextual appropriateness. Drawing on these findings, we conclude by outlining directions for future research aimed at developing AR-based assistive solutions tailored to the lunar setting.
summary: Future astronauts living and working on the Moon will face extreme environmental conditions impeding their operational safety and performance. While it has been suggested that Augmented Reality (AR) Head-Up Displays (HUDs) could potentially help mitigate some of these adversities, the applicability of AR in the unique lunar context remains underexplored. To address this limitation, we have produced an accurate representation of the lunar setting in virtual reality (VR) which then formed our testbed for the exploration of prospective operational scenarios with aerospace experts. Herein we present findings based on qualitative reflections made by the first 6 study participants. AR was found instrumental in several use cases, including the support of navigation and risk awareness. Major design challenges were likewise identified, including the importance of redundancy and contextual appropriateness. Drawing on these findings, we conclude by outlining directions for future research aimed at developing AR-based assistive solutions tailored to the lunar setting.
abstract_zh: 未来在月球上生活和工作的宇航员将面临极端的环境条件，影响他们的操作安全和表现。虽然有人认为增强现实 (AR) 平视显示器 (HUD) 可能有助于缓解其中一些逆境，但 AR 在独特的月球环境中的适用性仍未得到充分探索。为了解决这一限制，我们在虚拟现实 (VR) 中准确呈现了月球环境，从而形成了我们与航空航天专家一起探索前瞻性操作场景的测试平台。在此，我们根据前 6 名研究参与者的定性反思提出研究结果。 AR 在多个用例中发挥了重要作用，包括支持导航和风险意识。同样确定了主要的设计挑战，包括冗余和上下文适当性的重要性。根据这些发现，我们总结了未来的研究方向，旨在开发适合月球环境的基于增强现实的辅助解决方案。

AI.tldr: 本文通过虚拟现实测试床探讨增强现实（AR）技术在月球环境下支持人类操作的潜力。
AI.motivation: 月球极端环境对宇航员操作安全和效率构成挑战，而AR抬头显示器（HUD）的应用潜力尚未在月球场景中充分研究。
AI.method: 构建高精度月球环境虚拟现实测试床，邀请航天专家参与6名研究对象的定性实验，分析AR在操作场景中的适用性。
AI.result: AR在导航辅助和风险预警等场景中表现显著，但需解决冗余设计和情境适配性等关键挑战。
AI.conclusion: 未来研究应聚焦开发适配月球环境的AR辅助系统，需结合实验发现优化技术设计与应用场景。

authors_text: Vishnu Pandi Chellapandi,Liangqi Yuan,Stanislaw H /. Zak,Ziran Wang
title: A Survey of Federated Learning for Connected and Automated Vehicles
title_zh: 互联和自动化车辆联合学习调查
abstract: Connected and Automated Vehicles (CAVs) are one of the emerging technologies in the automotive domain that has the potential to alleviate the issues of accidents, traffic congestion, and pollutant emissions, leading to a safe, efficient, and sustainable transportation system. Machine learning-based methods are widely used in CAVs for crucial tasks like perception, motion planning, and motion control, where machine learning models in CAVs are solely trained using the local vehicle data, and the performance is not certain when exposed to new environments or unseen conditions. Federated learning (FL) is an effective solution for CAVs that enables a collaborative model development with multiple vehicles in a distributed learning framework. FL enables CAVs to learn from a wide range of driving environments and improve their overall performance while ensuring the privacy and security of local vehicle data. In this paper, we review the progress accomplished by researchers in applying FL to CAVs. A broader view of the various data modalities and algorithms that have been implemented on CAVs is provided. Specific applications of FL are reviewed in detail, and an analysis of the challenges and future scope of research are presented.
summary: Connected and Automated Vehicles (CAVs) are one of the emerging technologies in the automotive domain that has the potential to alleviate the issues of accidents, traffic congestion, and pollutant emissions, leading to a safe, efficient, and sustainable transportation system. Machine learning-based methods are widely used in CAVs for crucial tasks like perception, motion planning, and motion control, where machine learning models in CAVs are solely trained using the local vehicle data, and the performance is not certain when exposed to new environments or unseen conditions. Federated learning (FL) is an effective solution for CAVs that enables a collaborative model development with multiple vehicles in a distributed learning framework. FL enables CAVs to learn from a wide range of driving environments and improve their overall performance while ensuring the privacy and security of local vehicle data. In this paper, we review the progress accomplished by researchers in applying FL to CAVs. A broader view of the various data modalities and algorithms that have been implemented on CAVs is provided. Specific applications of FL are reviewed in detail, and an analysis of the challenges and future scope of research are presented.
abstract_zh: 联网和自动驾驶汽车（CAV）是汽车领域的新兴技术之一，有潜力缓解事故、交通拥堵和污染物排放问题，从而形成安全、高效和可持续的交通系统。基于机器学习的方法广泛应用于 CAV 中，用于执行感知、运动规划和运动控制等关键任务，其中 CAV 中的机器学习模型仅使用本地车辆数据进行训练，并且在暴露于新环境或未见条件时性能不确定。联邦学习 (FL) 是一种有效的 CAV 解决方案，可在分布式学习框架中实现多车辆的协作模型开发。 FL 使 CAV 能够从各种驾驶环境中学习并提高其整体性能，同时确保本地车辆数据的隐私和安全。在本文中，我们回顾了研究人员将 FL 应用到 CAV 方​​面所取得的进展。提供了对 CAV 上实施的各种数据模式和算法的更广泛的了解。详细回顾了 FL 的具体应用，并分析了挑战和未来的研究范围。

AI.tldr: 综述联邦学习在车联网和自动驾驶车辆中的应用进展、挑战及未来方向
AI.motivation: 解决CAVs中本地数据训练模型泛化能力不足的问题，并保障数据隐私
AI.method: 系统回顾现有文献，分析不同数据模态、算法及具体应用案例
AI.result: 总结FL在CAVs中的应用现状，识别技术挑战并提出未来研究方向
AI.conclusion: FL能提升CAVs性能，但需克服通信开销、异构性等挑战

search_text: A Survey of Federated Learning for Connected and Automated Vehicles 互联和自动化车辆联合学习调查 Connected and Automated Vehicles (CAVs) are one of the emerging technologies in the automotive domain that has the potential to alleviate the issues of accidents, traffic congestion, and pollutant emissions, leading to a safe, efficient, and sustainable transportation system. Machine learning-based methods are widely used in CAVs for crucial tasks like perception, motion planning, and motion control, where machine learning models in CAVs are solely trained using the local vehicle data, and the performance is not certain when exposed to new environments or unseen conditions. Federated learning (FL) is an effective solution for CAVs that enables a collaborative model development with multiple vehicles in a distributed learning framework. FL enables CAVs to learn from a wide range of driving environments and improve their overall performance while ensuring the privacy and security of local vehicle data. In this paper, we review the progress accomplished by researchers in applying FL to CAVs. A broader view of the various data modalities and algorithms that have been implemented on CAVs is provided. Specific applications of FL are reviewed in detail, and an analysis of the challenges and future scope of research are presented. 联网和自动驾驶汽车（CAV）是汽车领域的新兴技术之一，有潜力缓解事故、交通拥堵和污染物排放问题，从而形成安全、高效和可持续的交通系统。基于机器学习的方法广泛应用于 CAV 中，用于执行感知、运动规划和运动控制等关键任务，其中 CAV 中的机器学习模型仅使用本地车辆数据进行训练，并且在暴露于新环境或未见条件时性能不确定。联邦学习 (FL) 是一种有效的 CAV 解决方案，可在分布式学习框架中实现多车辆的协作模型开发。 FL 使 CAV 能够从各种驾驶环境中学习并提高其整体性能，同时确保本地车辆数据的隐私和安全。在本文中，我们回顾了研究人员将 FL 应用到 CAV 方​​面所取得的进展。提供了对 CAV 上实施的各种数据模式和算法的更广泛的了解。详细回顾了 FL 的具体应用，并分析了挑战和未来的研究范围。 综述联邦学习在车联网和自动驾驶车辆中的应用进展、挑战及未来方向 Vishnu Pandi Chellapandi,Liangqi Yuan,Stanislaw H /. Zak,Ziran Wang cs.LG,cs.RO

authors_text: Hongmeng Liu,Jiapeng Zhao,Yixuan Huo,Yuyan Wang,Chun Liao,Liyan Shen,Shiyao Cui,Jinqiao Shi
title: URM4DMU: an user represention model for darknet markets users
title_zh: URM4DMU：暗网市场用户的用户表示模型
abstract: Darknet markets provide a large platform for trading illicit goods and services due to their anonymity. Learning an invariant representation of each user based on their posts on different markets makes it easy to aggregate user information across different platforms, which helps identify anonymous users. Traditional user representation methods mainly rely on modeling the text information of posts and cannot capture the temporal content and the forum interaction of posts. While recent works mainly use CNN to model the text information of posts, failing to effectively model posts whose length changes frequently in an episode. To address the above problems, we propose a model named URM4DMU(User Representation Model for Darknet Markets Users) which mainly improves the post representation by augmenting convolutional operators and self-attention with an adaptive gate mechanism. It performs much better when combined with the temporal content and the forum interaction of posts. We demonstrate the effectiveness of URM4DMU on four darknet markets. The average improvements on MRR value and Recall@10 are 22.5% and 25.5% over the state-of-the-art method respectively.
summary: Darknet markets provide a large platform for trading illicit goods and services due to their anonymity. Learning an invariant representation of each user based on their posts on different markets makes it easy to aggregate user information across different platforms, which helps identify anonymous users. Traditional user representation methods mainly rely on modeling the text information of posts and cannot capture the temporal content and the forum interaction of posts. While recent works mainly use CNN to model the text information of posts, failing to effectively model posts whose length changes frequently in an episode. To address the above problems, we propose a model named URM4DMU(User Representation Model for Darknet Markets Users) which mainly improves the post representation by augmenting convolutional operators and self-attention with an adaptive gate mechanism. It performs much better when combined with the temporal content and the forum interaction of posts. We demonstrate the effectiveness of URM4DMU on four darknet markets. The average improvements on MRR value and Recall@10 are 22.5% and 25.5% over the state-of-the-art method respectively.
abstract_zh: 由于其匿名性，暗网市场为非法商品和服务交易提供了一个大型平台。根据每个用户在不同市场上的帖子学习其不变的表示，可以轻松地跨不同平台聚合用户信息，这有助于识别匿名用户。传统的用户表示方法主要依赖于对帖子的文本信息进行建模，无法捕获帖子的时间内容和论坛交互。虽然最近的工作主要使用CNN对帖子的文本信息进行建模，但未能有效地对每集长度频繁变化的帖子进行建模。为了解决上述问题，我们提出了一种名为 URM4DMU（暗网市场用户的用户表示模型）的模型，该模型主要通过增强卷积算子和自适应门机制的自注意力来改进后表示。当与帖子的时间内容和论坛互动相结合时，它的表现会更好。我们展示了 URM4DMU 在四个暗网市场上的有效性。 MRR 值和 Recall@10 的平均改进分别比最先进的方法高 22.5% 和 25.5%。

AI.tldr: 构建用于暗网市场用户的用户表示模型，以跨平台聚合用户信息并识别匿名用户。
AI.motivation: 传统方法无法有效捕捉帖子的时间动态和论坛互动，且现有CNN方法难以处理变长帖子。
AI.method: 提出URM4DMU模型，通过增强卷积运算和自注意力机制，并结合自适应门控机制改进帖子表示，结合时间内容和论坛互动。
AI.result: 在四个暗网市场数据集上，MRR和Recall@10指标分别平均提升22.5%和25.5%。
AI.conclusion: URM4DMU在建模变长帖子和动态信息方面更有效，为暗网用户分析提供了改进方法。

authors_text: Joseph Farrington,Kezhi Li,Wai Keong Wong,Martin Utley
title: Going faster to see further: GPU-accelerated value iteration and simulation for perishable inventory control using JAX
title_zh: 更快看得更远：使用 JAX 进行易腐烂库存控制的 GPU 加速值迭代和模拟
abstract: Value iteration can find the optimal replenishment policy for a perishable inventory problem, but is computationally demanding due to the large state spaces that are required to represent the age profile of stock. The parallel processing capabilities of modern GPUs can reduce the wall time required to run value iteration by updating many states simultaneously. The adoption of GPU-accelerated approaches has been limited in operational research relative to other fields like machine learning, in which new software frameworks have made GPU programming widely accessible. We used the Python library JAX to implement value iteration and simulators of the underlying Markov decision processes in a high-level API, and relied on this library's function transformations and compiler to efficiently utilize GPU hardware. Our method can extend use of value iteration to settings that were previously considered infeasible or impractical. We demonstrate this on example scenarios from three recent studies which include problems with over 16 million states and additional problem features, such as substitution between products, that increase computational complexity. We compare the performance of the optimal replenishment policies to heuristic policies, fitted using simulation optimization in JAX which allowed the parallel evaluation of multiple candidate policy parameters on thousands of simulated years. The heuristic policies gave a maximum optimality gap of 2.49%. Our general approach may be applicable to a wide range of problems in operational research that would benefit from large-scale parallel computation on consumer-grade GPU hardware.
summary: Value iteration can find the optimal replenishment policy for a perishable inventory problem, but is computationally demanding due to the large state spaces that are required to represent the age profile of stock. The parallel processing capabilities of modern GPUs can reduce the wall time required to run value iteration by updating many states simultaneously. The adoption of GPU-accelerated approaches has been limited in operational research relative to other fields like machine learning, in which new software frameworks have made GPU programming widely accessible. We used the Python library JAX to implement value iteration and simulators of the underlying Markov decision processes in a high-level API, and relied on this library's function transformations and compiler to efficiently utilize GPU hardware. Our method can extend use of value iteration to settings that were previously considered infeasible or impractical. We demonstrate this on example scenarios from three recent studies which include problems with over 16 million states and additional problem features, such as substitution between products, that increase computational complexity. We compare the performance of the optimal replenishment policies to heuristic policies, fitted using simulation optimization in JAX which allowed the parallel evaluation of multiple candidate policy parameters on thousands of simulated years. The heuristic policies gave a maximum optimality gap of 2.49%. Our general approach may be applicable to a wide range of problems in operational research that would benefit from large-scale parallel computation on consumer-grade GPU hardware.
abstract_zh: 值迭代可以为易腐烂的库存问题找到最佳补货策略，但由于需要较大的状态空间来表示库存的寿命概况，因此计算要求较高。现代 GPU 的并行处理能力可以通过同时更新许多状态来减少运行值迭代所需的时间。相对于机器学习等其他领域，GPU 加速方法的采用在运筹学领域受到限制，在机器学习等领域，新的软件框架使 GPU 编程变得更容易实现。我们使用Python库JAX在高级API中实现底层马尔可夫决策过程的值迭代和模拟器，并依靠该库的函数转换和编译器来有效地利用GPU硬件。我们的方法可以将值迭代的使用扩展到以前被认为不可行或不切实际的设置。我们在最近三项研究的示例场景中证明了这一点，其中包括超过 1600 万个状态的问题和其他问题特征，例如产品之间的替换，这增加了计算复杂性。我们将最佳补给政策的性能与启发式政策进行比较，使用 JAX 中的模拟优化进行拟合，这允许在数千个模拟年中并行评估多个候选政策参数。启发式策略的最大最优性差距为 2.49%。我们的通用方法可能适用于运筹学中的各种问题，这些问题将受益于消费级 GPU 硬件上的大规模并行计算。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用JAX库实现基于GPU加速的价值迭代算法，以解决易腐库存控制中的大规模状态空间计算问题。
AI.motivation: 传统价值迭代方法因需处理大规模状态空间而计算耗时，而GPU并行计算能力在运筹学领域应用不足，亟需通过高效框架提升计算效率。
AI.method: 采用JAX库的高级API和编译器功能，结合GPU并行处理能力，实现价值迭代算法及马尔可夫决策过程模拟器，高效利用硬件资源。
AI.result: 方法成功应用于包含1600万状态及产品替代等复杂特征的场景，启发式策略与最优策略的最优性差距最大为2.49%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过消费级GPU的大规模并行计算，为运筹学领域提供了可扩展的高效解决方案，具有广泛的应用潜力。

authors_text: Ho-Hsiang Wu,Oriol Nieto,Juan Pablo Bello,Justin Salamon
title: Audio-Text Models Do Not Yet Leverage Natural Language
title_zh: 音频文本模型尚未利用自然语言

abstract: Multi-modal contrastive learning techniques in the audio-text domain have quickly become a highly active area of research. Most works are evaluated with standard audio retrieval and classification benchmarks assuming that (i) these models are capable of leveraging the rich information contained in natural language, and (ii) current benchmarks are able to capture the nuances of such information. In this work, we show that state-of-the-art audio-text models do not yet really understand natural language, especially contextual concepts such as sequential or concurrent ordering of sound events. Our results suggest that existing benchmarks are not sufficient to assess these models' capabilities to match complex contexts from the audio and text modalities. We propose a Transformer-based architecture and show that, unlike prior work, it is capable of modeling the sequential relationship between sound events in the text and audio, given appropriate benchmark data. We advocate for the collection or generation of additional, diverse, data to allow future research to fully leverage natural language for audio-text modeling.
summary: Multi-modal contrastive learning techniques in the audio-text domain have quickly become a highly active area of research. Most works are evaluated with standard audio retrieval and classification benchmarks assuming that (i) these models are capable of leveraging the rich information contained in natural language, and (ii) current benchmarks are able to capture the nuances of such information. In this work, we show that state-of-the-art audio-text models do not yet really understand natural language, especially contextual concepts such as sequential or concurrent ordering of sound events. Our results suggest that existing benchmarks are not sufficient to assess these models' capabilities to match complex contexts from the audio and text modalities. We propose a Transformer-based architecture and show that, unlike prior work, it is capable of modeling the sequential relationship between sound events in the text and audio, given appropriate benchmark data. We advocate for the collection or generation of additional, diverse, data to allow future research to fully leverage natural language for audio-text modeling.
abstract_zh: 音频文本领域的多模态对比学习技术已迅速成为一个高度活跃的研究领域。大多数作品都是使用标准音频检索和分类基准进行评估的，假设（i）这些模型能够利用自然语言中包含的丰富信息，并且（ii）当前基准能够捕获此类信息的细微差别。在这项工作中，我们表明最先进的音频文本模型尚未真正理解自然语言，特别是诸如声音事件的顺序或并发排序之类的上下文概念。我们的结果表明，现有的基准不足以评估这些模型匹配音频和文本模式的复杂上下文的能力。我们提出了一种基于 Transformer 的架构，并表明，与之前的工作不同，它能够在给定适当的基准数据的情况下对文本和音频中的声音事件之间的顺序关系进行建模。我们主张收集或生成额外的、多样化的数据，以便未来的研究能够充分利用自然语言进行音频文本建模。

AI.tldr: 本文指出当前音频-文本模型未能有效利用自然语言中的上下文信息，并提出基于Transformer的改进方法。
AI.motivation: 现有音频-文本模型和基准测试无法准确评估模型对自然语言复杂上下文（如声音事件顺序）的理解能力。
AI.method: 设计基于Transformer的架构，通过建模文本与音频中声音事件的时序关系来改进对自然语言的理解。
AI.result: 实验表明现有基准不足，而新方法在适当数据下能有效捕捉声音事件的时序关联。
AI.conclusion: 需收集更多样化的数据以推动音频-文本模型充分挖掘自然语言的潜力。
